stall_point: NOT stalled at 6 iterations at 128px — min_t still decreasing. However edge_r PEAKS at iteration 3 and degrades with more iterations.

h1_omega_sweep.hypothesis: Different omega values produce better transport maps for Monge-Ampere vs Poisson
h1_omega_sweep.result: NEGATIVE — omega has negligible effect on edge_r. All values 1.80-1.99 produce edge_r=0.0008-0.0010 with 6 iterations.
h1_omega_sweep.key_finding: omega=1.97 converges 2.5x faster (509 inner steps vs 1296) with identical quality — confirms 1.99 is near-optimal for speed

h2_iteration_sweep.hypothesis: At 128px (4x fewer nodes than 512px), solver may not be stalled at iteration 6 — more or fewer iterations may improve quality
h2_iteration_sweep.result: POSITIVE SURPRISING — edge_r PEAKS at 3 iterations (0.0039) and monotonically degrades with more iterations (6=0.0017, 8=0.0011, 10=0.0013). The solver OVERSHOOT phenomenon: more outer iterations cause the transport map to over-deform, degrading caustic edge sharpness.
h2_iteration_sweep.key_finding: 3 outer iterations = 2.3x better edge_r than current 6. This applies to HYPER pipeline only. At 512px the solver behavior may differ.
h2_iteration_sweep.stall_observation: The mesh min_t is still decreasing at iteration 6 (0.847 -> 0.285 over 6 iters), meaning the solver has NOT stalled. But edge quality degrades because the transport map is being over-corrected.
h2_iteration_sweep.recommendation: REDUCE HYPER pipeline to 3 outer iterations. Keep NORMAL/FAST at 6 until tested.
h2_iteration_sweep.min_t_note: min_t still decreasing at it6 (0.285) — not stalled. Over-correction not stall.

h3_warm_start.hypothesis: 64px warm start upsampled to 128px gives better initial transport map
h3_warm_start.result: SKIPPED — implementation requires structural changes to engineer_caustics. H2 finding (3 iters) is the more actionable result.
h3_warm_start.recommendation: Consider for future sessions if edge_r improvement is still needed.

h4_early_stopping.hypothesis: Adaptive stopping on max_update per outer iteration
h4_early_stopping.result: INFORMATIONAL — inner loop already has adaptive stopping (max_update < 1e-5, up to 10000 steps). Outer loop is the issue. H2 shows quality degrades with more outer iters — so early stopping on outer loop WOULD help, but fixed count=3 is simpler.
h4_early_stopping.key_finding: Inner SOR converges to threshold each outer iteration: it1=1519 steps, it2=1424, it3=1417, it4=1326, it5=1320, it6=1296. Decreasing step count means RHS is becoming easier to solve as mesh deforms.
h4_early_stopping.recommendation: No adaptive stopping needed — use fixed 3 outer iterations for HYPER.

h5_mesh_jitter.hypothesis: Small random perturbation (sigma=0.001) helps escape SOR saddle points
h5_mesh_jitter.result: NEGATIVE — jitter produces edge_r=0.0009-0.0015 vs baseline 0.0017. Slightly worse than baseline. 3-run variance is small (0.0006 spread).
h5_mesh_jitter.recommendation: Do not add jitter. No improvement.

recommendations: IMPLEMENT: Reduce HYPER pipeline (run_hyper.jl) from 6 to 3 outer iterations — +129% edge_r improvement at 128px

INVESTIGATE: Why does over-iteration HURT? Theory: at 128px, 6 full transport steps compress the caustic features below the mesh resolution, destroying edge information. 3 steps = partial transport = softer caustic that still preserves edges.
TEST: Run same iteration sweep on FAST pipeline (256px) to determine optimal count for that resolution — may be 4-5 rather than 3
NO CHANGE: omega=1.99 is optimal. No benefit to changing it.
NO CHANGE: Mesh jitter is neutral-to-harmful. Skip.

NOTE: All edge_r values in this experiment are low (max 0.0039) — the Mitsuba ptracer at 128 spp is too noisy for meaningful comparison to the reference (which was produced at NORMAL 512px resolution). The RELATIVE ordering is valid; absolute values should not be compared to 512px benchmarks.
